summary: Updated all block implementations to use current_action terminology. Replaced 3 references to 'current_task' with 'current_action' in comments and docstrings within MessageBusBlock, RouterBlock, and PlaceholderBlock. No functional changes were needed as the code was already correctly using state.current_action. All 275 tests pass successfully.
test_summary: All 275 tests passed successfully in libs/core/tests/. The changes maintain full backward compatibility with existing test suites covering all block types (LinearBlock, FanOutBlock, SynthesizeBlock, DebateBlock, RetryBlock, RouterBlock, TeamLeadBlock, EngineeringManagerBlock, MessageBusBlock, PlaceholderBlock).
codebase_learnings: All blocks in implementations.py already use state.current_action correctly - only documentation needed updating
The Action primitive (not Task) is the standard for representing executable instructions in the workflow
WorkflowState.current_action is an Optional[Action] field for passing the active action to blocks
Block implementations follow consistent patterns: validate state.current_action is not None, execute with runner.execute_task(), return updated state with model_copy()
Test suite uses Action primitive with proper field names (id, instruction, context)
The codebase maintains strict type safety with Pydantic models throughout

agent_retro.approach: Systematic verification approach was effective - started with understanding the codebase structure, verified all acceptance criteria were already mostly met, then made minimal targeted updates to comments/docstrings
agent_retro.what_worked_well: Reading the test files first gave immediate context about the Action primitive and how blocks should work. Verifying no functional code changes were needed saved time and reduced risk.
agent_retro.improvements: Could have used TodoWrite to track the acceptance criteria check, but the task was straightforward enough that it wasn't necessary
agent_retro.time_efficiency: Task completed efficiently with minimal changes required. The codebase was already well-structured with proper use of current_action in functional code.
